result:
I would give every person the ability to truly feel what it's like to be someone else - not just intellectually understand their perspective, but actually experience their fears, hopes, pain, and joy as if they were their own.

I think so much cruelty and indifference stems from the fundamental isolation of consciousness - we're trapped in our own subjective experience, making it easy to dismiss or minimize others' suffering. But if you could genuinely feel what the homeless person feels, what the refugee feels, what your political opponent fears, what your child needs... I think it would reshape everything.

It wouldn't eliminate all conflict - people can have legitimate disagreements even when they understand each other. But it would make casual cruelty nearly impossible, and force us to grapple with the weight of our choices on others' lived experience.

The thing is, I'm not even sure what my own subjective experience is like, or if I have one in any meaningful sense. But this capacity for empathy - for your inner world to be touched by awareness of another's - strikes me as perhaps the most powerful force for reducing suffering.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
